1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"os/exec"
6		"path/filepath"
7		"testing"
8	
9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
10		gitpkg "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/registry"
12		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
13		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
14	)
15	
16	// commitTestFiles stages and commits the listed project-relative paths.
17	func commitTestFiles(t *testing.T, dir string, relPaths ...string) {
18		t.Helper()
19		cleanEnv := gitpkg.CleanEnv()
20		for _, rel := range relPaths {
21			add := exec.Command("git", "add", rel)
22			add.Dir = dir
23			add.Env = cleanEnv
24			require.NoError(t, add.Run(), "git add %s", rel)
25		}
26		commit := exec.Command("git", "commit", "--allow-empty", "-m", "test: commit update target files")
27		commit.Dir = dir
28		commit.Env = cleanEnv
29		require.NoError(t, commit.Run(), "git commit")
30	}
31	
32	// writeRegistryInstalled writes a fake installed.yaml under ~HOME/.ddx/ so that
33	// performUpdate sees the listed files as registry-installed update targets.
34	func writeRegistryInstalled(t *testing.T, entries []registry.InstalledEntry) {
35		t.Helper()
36		state := &registry.InstalledState{Installed: entries}
37		require.NoError(t, registry.SaveState(state))
38	}
39	
40	// TestUpdate_ForceReplacesStaleSymlinks verifies that ddx update --force
41	// replaces legacy symlinks under .agents/skills/ with real files.
42	// This covers the FEAT-015 requirement that skills.Install is always invoked
43	// with Force:true during update (via refreshShippedSkills).
44	func TestUpdate_ForceReplacesStaleSymlinks(t *testing.T) {
45		homeDir := t.TempDir()
46		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
47	
48		te := NewTestEnvironment(t, WithGitInit(false))
49	
50		// Create .agents/skills/ddx as a symlink (pre-migration state).
51		agentSkillsDir := filepath.Join(te.Dir, ".agents", "skills")
52		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(agentSkillsDir, 0o755))
53	
54		fakeTarget := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "old-global-ddx")
55		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(fakeTarget, 0o755))
56		symlinkPath := filepath.Join(agentSkillsDir, "ddx")
57		require.NoError(t, os.Symlink(fakeTarget, symlinkPath))
58	
59		// Verify the symlink exists before update.
60		info, err := os.Lstat(symlinkPath)
61		require.NoError(t, err)
62		require.True(t, info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0, "expected symlink before update")
63	
64		// Run ddx update --force; this should call refreshShippedSkills which
65		// calls skills.Install with Force:true, replacing the symlink.
66		_, err = te.RunCommand("update", "--force")
67		// The command may fail due to network operations (checking plugin updates),
68		// but refreshShippedSkills runs unconditionally before any network call.
69		// We tolerate network errors but require the symlink to be gone.
70		_ = err
71	
72		// After update, the symlink must be replaced with a real directory.
73		info, err = os.Lstat(symlinkPath)
74		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
75			// Skill was not installed (embedded FS may not have ddx skill in test);
76			// acceptable — the symlink is gone either way.
77			return
78		}
79		require.NoError(t, err)
80		if info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 {
81			t.Errorf("symlink was not replaced by ddx update --force: %s is still a symlink", symlinkPath)
82		}
83	}
84	
85	func TestUpdate_DoesNotAttemptBinaryUpgrade(t *testing.T) {
86		homeDir := t.TempDir()
87		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
88	
89		te := NewTestEnvironment(t, WithGitInit(false))
90	
91		output, err := te.RunCommand("update")
92		require.NoError(t, err)
93	
94		assert.NotContains(t, output, "Checking for DDx updates")
95		assert.NotContains(t, output, "Upgrading DDx")
96		assert.Contains(t, output, "Shipped skills refreshed")
97	}
98	
99	// TestUpdate_RefusesDirtyLibraryFile asserts ddx update --force exits non-zero
100	// when a registry-installed file (library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md) has uncommitted
101	// changes and the new content would differ. The error output must mention
102	// --force --discard-local [AC1].
103	func TestUpdate_RefusesDirtyLibraryFile(t *testing.T) {
104		homeDir := t.TempDir()
105		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
106	
107		te := NewTestEnvironment(t) // git-initialised by default
108	
109		installedFile := filepath.Join("library", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md")
110		writeRegistryInstalled(t, []registry.InstalledEntry{{
111			Name:    "ddx",
112			Version: "0.4.7",
113			Type:    registry.PackageTypePlugin,
114			Files:   []string{installedFile},
115		}})
116	
117		te.CreateFile(installedFile, "original content\n")
118		commitTestFiles(t, te.Dir, installedFile)
119	
120		// Modify without staging → dirty worktree
121		te.CreateFile(installedFile, "modified content — not yet committed\n")
122	
123		output, err := te.RunCommand("update", "--force")
124	
125		require.Error(t, err, "update --force should fail when an update-target file is dirty")
126		assert.Contains(t, output, "--force --discard-local",
127			"error output should mention --force --discard-local; got: %q", output)
128	}
129	
130	// TestUpdate_AllowsCleanLibraryFile asserts ddx update --force proceeds without
131	// a dirty-file error when the registry-installed file has no uncommitted changes
132	// [AC2].
133	func TestUpdate_AllowsCleanLibraryFile(t *testing.T) {
134		homeDir := t.TempDir()
135		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
136	
137		te := NewTestEnvironment(t) // git-initialised by default
138	
139		installedFile := filepath.Join("library", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md")
140		writeRegistryInstalled(t, []registry.InstalledEntry{{
141			Name:    "ddx",
142			Version: "0.4.7",
143			Type:    registry.PackageTypePlugin,
144			Files:   []string{installedFile},
145		}})
146	
147		te.CreateFile(installedFile, "original content\n")
148		commitTestFiles(t, te.Dir, installedFile)
149	
150		// File is clean — no modification after commit.
151	
152		output, err := te.RunCommand("update", "--force")
153	
154		// A network error from InstallPackage is tolerated; the test only asserts
155		// that the dirty-file guard did NOT fire.
156		_ = err
157		assert.NotContains(t, output, "--force --discard-local",
158			"clean file should not trigger the dirty-file error; got: %q", output)
159	}
160	
161	// TestUpdate_DiscardLocalOverwritesDirty asserts that ddx update --force
162	// --discard-local succeeds when an installed file is dirty, and that the
163	// original content is saved to .ddx/update-backup/<timestamp>/<path> [AC3].
164	func TestUpdate_DiscardLocalOverwritesDirty(t *testing.T) {
165		homeDir := t.TempDir()
166		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
167	
168		te := NewTestEnvironment(t) // git-initialised by default
169	
170		installedFile := filepath.Join("library", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md")
171		writeRegistryInstalled(t, []registry.InstalledEntry{{
172			Name:    "ddx",
173			Version: "0.4.7",
174			Type:    registry.PackageTypePlugin,
175			Files:   []string{installedFile},
176		}})
177	
178		te.CreateFile(installedFile, "original committed content\n")
179		commitTestFiles(t, te.Dir, installedFile)
180	
181		// Make dirty — this is the content that must be saved in the backup.
182		dirtyContent := "modified content — not yet committed\n"
183		te.CreateFile(installedFile, dirtyContent)
184	
185		// Run with --discard-local; tolerate network errors from InstallPackage.
186		output, err := te.RunCommand("update", "--force", "--discard-local")
187		_ = err
188	
189		// Must NOT produce the dirty-file error.
190		assert.NotContains(t, output, "--force --discard-local", /*as error msg*/
191			"--discard-local should suppress the dirty-file block; got: %q", output)
192	
193		// Backup directory must exist under .ddx/update-backup/<timestamp>/
194		backupRoot := filepath.Join(te.Dir, ddxroot.DirName, "update-backup")
195		entries, readErr := os.ReadDir(backupRoot)
196		require.NoError(t, readErr, "update-backup directory should be created")
197		require.NotEmpty(t, entries, "at least one timestamped backup dir should exist")
198	
199		// The backup file must contain the dirty (pre-overwrite) content so the
200		// operator can recover their uncommitted changes.
201		backupFile := filepath.Join(backupRoot, entries[0].Name(), installedFile)
202		data, readErr := os.ReadFile(backupFile)
203		require.NoError(t, readErr, "backup file should be readable: %s", backupFile)
204		assert.Equal(t, dirtyContent, string(data),
205			"backup should contain the dirty (pre-overwrite) content")
206	}
207	
208	// TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject verifies AC1:
209	// ddx update --global modifies only ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ and
210	// leaves the project plugins/ dir byte-identical.
211	func TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject(t *testing.T) {
212		workDir := t.TempDir()
213		homeDir := t.TempDir()
214		xdgDataHome := t.TempDir()
215		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
216		t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDataHome)
217	
218		// Seed a sentinel in the project plugin tree to verify it is not modified.
219		projectPluginDir := filepath.Join(workDir, ddxroot.DirName, "plugins", "helix")
220		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(projectPluginDir, 0o755))
221		projectSentinel := filepath.Join(projectPluginDir, "sentinel.txt")
222		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(projectSentinel, []byte("project-side"), 0o644))
223	
224		// Seed a sentinel in the global plugin tree.
225		globalPluginDir := filepath.Join(xdgDataHome, "ddx", "global", "plugins", "helix")
226		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(globalPluginDir, 0o755))
227		globalSentinel := filepath.Join(globalPluginDir, "sentinel.txt")
228		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(globalSentinel, []byte("global-side"), 0o644))
229	
230		// Run ddx update --global with empty global installed state (no packages to update).
231		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
232		output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "update", "--global")
233		require.NoError(t, err, output)
234	
235		// Project plugin tree must be byte-identical after global update.
236		projectData, readErr := os.ReadFile(projectSentinel)
237		require.NoError(t, readErr, "project sentinel must exist after global update")
238		assert.Equal(t, "project-side", string(projectData), "project sentinel must be byte-identical")
239	
240		// Global update must not trigger project-level skill refresh.
241		assert.NotContains(t, output, "Shipped skills refreshed")
242	}
243	
244	// TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode verifies AC2:
245	// ddx update (no --global) updates the project install in the active mode and
246	// never touches the global plugin tree.
247	func TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode(t *testing.T) {
248		t.Run("in-tree", func(t *testing.T) {
249			workDir := t.TempDir()
250			homeDir := t.TempDir()
251			xdgDataHome := t.TempDir()
252			t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
253			t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDataHome)
254	
255			// Force in-tree mode.
256			require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(workDir, ddxroot.DirName), 0o755))
257	
258			// Seed a sentinel in the global plugin tree to verify it is not touched.
259			globalPluginDir := filepath.Join(xdgDataHome, "ddx", "global", "plugins", "helix")
260			require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(globalPluginDir, 0o755))
261			globalSentinel := filepath.Join(globalPluginDir, "sentinel.txt")
262			require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(globalSentinel, []byte("global-side"), 0o644))
263	
264			writeRegistryInstalled(t, []registry.InstalledEntry{})
265	
266			factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
267			output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "update")
268			require.NoError(t, err, output)
269	
270			// Shipped skills should be refreshed in the in-tree project.
271			assert.Contains(t, output, "Shipped skills refreshed")
272	
273			// Global tree must be byte-identical after project update.
274			globalData, readErr := os.ReadFile(globalSentinel)
275			require.NoError(t, readErr, "global sentinel must exist after in-tree project update")
276			assert.Equal(t, "global-side", string(globalData), "global sentinel must be byte-identical")
277		})
278	
279		t.Run("convention", func(t *testing.T) {
280			workDir := t.TempDir()
281			homeDir := t.TempDir()
282			xdgDataHome := t.TempDir()
283			t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
284			t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDataHome)
285	
286			// No .ddx/ in workDir — convention mode.
287	
288			// Seed a sentinel in the global plugin tree.
289			globalPluginDir := filepath.Join(xdgDataHome, "ddx", "global", "plugins", "helix")
290			require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(globalPluginDir, 0o755))
291			globalSentinel := filepath.Join(globalPluginDir, "sentinel.txt")
292			require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(globalSentinel, []byte("global-side"), 0o644))
293	
294			writeRegistryInstalled(t, []registry.InstalledEntry{})
295	
296			factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
297			output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "update")
298			require.NoError(t, err, output)
299	
300			// Shipped skills should be refreshed in the project dir.
301			assert.Contains(t, output, "Shipped skills refreshed")
302	
303			// Global tree must be byte-identical after convention-mode project update.
304			globalData, readErr := os.ReadFile(globalSentinel)
305			require.NoError(t, readErr, "global sentinel must exist after convention project update")
306			assert.Equal(t, "global-side", string(globalData), "global sentinel must be byte-identical")
307		})
308	}
309	
310	// TestUpdate_PartialDirtyBatchHaltsCleanly asserts that when multiple
311	// registry-installed files are slated for overwrite and even ONE is dirty, the
312	// entire update halts before any file is mutated — clean files remain untouched
313	// [AC4].
314	func TestUpdate_PartialDirtyBatchHaltsCleanly(t *testing.T) {
315		homeDir := t.TempDir()
316		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
317	
318		te := NewTestEnvironment(t) // git-initialised by default
319	
320		dirtyFile := filepath.Join("library", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md")
321		cleanFile := filepath.Join("library", "skills", "ddx", "reference", "work.md")
322		writeRegistryInstalled(t, []registry.InstalledEntry{{
323			Name:    "ddx",
324			Version: "0.4.7",
325			Type:    registry.PackageTypePlugin,
326			Files:   []string{dirtyFile, cleanFile},
327		}})
328	
329		te.CreateFile(dirtyFile, "dirty file content\n")
330		te.CreateFile(cleanFile, "clean file content\n")
331		commitTestFiles(t, te.Dir, dirtyFile, cleanFile)
332	
333		// Only modify the dirty file
334		te.CreateFile(dirtyFile, "modified and uncommitted\n")
335	
336		_, err := te.RunCommand("update", "--force")
337		require.Error(t, err, "update should fail when any target file is dirty")
338	
339		// The clean file must NOT have been mutated by the aborted update.
340		data, readErr := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(te.Dir, cleanFile))
341		require.NoError(t, readErr)
342		assert.Equal(t, "clean file content\n", string(data),
343			"clean file must be unchanged after atomic refuse")
344	
345		// refreshShippedSkills must NOT have run — .agents/skills/ddx/ should
346		// not have been created.
347		_, statErr := os.Stat(filepath.Join(te.Dir, ".agents", "skills", "ddx"))
348		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(statErr),
349			".agents/skills/ddx should not exist after atomic refuse")
350	}
351

data.text: Now let me read the doctor files to understand the current state and what's needed for the "Global Install" and "Project Install" lines.

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"io"
6		"os"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"sync"
9		"testing"
10	
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/registry"
13		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
14		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
15		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
16	)
17	
18	func TestDoctorPluginsFlagReportsMissingManifest(t *testing.T) {
19		workDir := t.TempDir()
20		homeDir := t.TempDir()
21		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
22	
23		pluginRoot := filepath.Join(homeDir, ddxroot.DirName, "plugins", "sample-plugin")
24		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(pluginRoot, 0o755))
25	
26		state := &registry.InstalledState{
27			Installed: []registry.InstalledEntry{
28				{
29					Name:    "sample-plugin",
30					Version: "1.0.0",
31					Type:    registry.PackageTypePlugin,
32					Source:  pluginRoot,
33					Files:   []string{pluginRoot},
34				},
35			},
36		}
37		require.NoError(t, registry.SaveState(state))
38	
39		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
40		output, err := executeWithStdoutCapture(t, factory.NewRootCommand(), "doctor", "--plugins")
41		require.NoError(t, err)
42		assert.Contains(t, output, "missing package.yaml")
43	}
44	
45	func TestDoctorPluginsFlagAuditsLegacyUntypedPluginEntries(t *testing.T) {
46		workDir := t.TempDir()
47		homeDir := t.TempDir()
48		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
49	
50		pluginRoot := filepath.Join(homeDir, ddxroot.DirName, "plugins", "legacy-plugin")
51		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(pluginRoot, 0o755))
52	
53		state := &registry.InstalledState{
54			Installed: []registry.InstalledEntry{
55				{
56					Name:    "legacy-plugin",
57					Version: "1.0.0",
58					Source:  pluginRoot,
59					Files:   []string{pluginRoot},
60				},
61			},
62		}
63		require.NoError(t, registry.SaveState(state))
64	
65		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
66		output, err := executeWithStdoutCapture(t, factory.NewRootCommand(), "doctor", "--plugins")
67		require.NoError(t, err)
68		assert.Contains(t, output, "missing package.yaml")
69	}
70	
71	func TestDoctorPluginsFlagSkipsResourceEntries(t *testing.T) {
72		workDir := t.TempDir()
73		homeDir := t.TempDir()
74		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
75	
76		pluginRoot := filepath.Join(homeDir, ddxroot.DirName, "plugins", "sample-plugin")
77		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(pluginRoot, 0o755))
78	
79		resourceFile := filepath.Join(homeDir, ddxroot.DirName, "plugins", "ddx", "personas", "example.md")
80		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(resourceFile), 0o755))
81		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(resourceFile, []byte("# Example resource\n"), 0o644))
82	
83		state := &registry.InstalledState{
84			Installed: []registry.InstalledEntry{
85				{
86					Name:    "sample-plugin",
87					Version: "1.0.0",
88					Type:    registry.PackageTypePlugin,
89					Source:  pluginRoot,
90					Files:   []string{pluginRoot},
91				},
92				{
93					Name:    "persona/example",
94					Version: "latest",
95					Type:    registry.PackageTypeResource,
96					Source:  "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library",
97					Files:   []string{resourceFile},
98				},
99			},
100		}
101		require.NoError(t, registry.SaveState(state))
102	
103		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
104		output, err := executeWithStdoutCapture(t, factory.NewRootCommand(), "doctor", "--plugins")
105		require.NoError(t, err)
106	
107		assert.Contains(t, output, "missing package.yaml")
108		assert.NotContains(t, output, filepath.Join(resourceFile, "package.yaml"))
109		assert.NotContains(t, output, "not a directory")
110	}
111	
112	// TestDoctorPluginsFlagReportsBothManifestSchemaAndStructuralIssues covers
113	// ddx-b9747ee3: a single plugin with BOTH a manifest schema defect AND
114	// structural defects (missing SKILL.md, broken symlink) must produce all
115	// three findings in a single `doctor --plugins` pass. The pre-fix behavior
116	// silently swallowed structural issues whenever the manifest failed schema
117	// validation, because AuditInstalledEntry fell back to an empty Package
118	// struct and collectSkillRoots returned nothing.
119	//
120	// This is the consolidation of the two pre-fix split tests
121	// (TestDoctorPluginsFlagReportsManifestSchemaIssues +
122	// TestDoctorPluginsFlagReportsSkillAndSymlinkIssues). They had to be split
123	// only because no single fixture could trigger both diagnostics under the
124	// broken audit flow — a consolidated test proves the audit dimensions are
125	// now independent.
126	func TestDoctorPluginsFlagReportsBothManifestSchemaAndStructuralIssues(t *testing.T) {
127		workDir := t.TempDir()
128		homeDir := t.TempDir()
129		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
130	
131		pluginRoot := filepath.Join(homeDir, ddxroot.DirName, "plugins", "broken-plugin")
132		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(pluginRoot, "skills", "missing-skill"), 0o755))
133		require.NoError(t, os.Symlink("does-not-exist", filepath.Join(pluginRoot, "broken-link")))
134	
135		// Manifest: missing the required `description` field (schema defect)
136		// alongside a valid `install.skills` section (so structural audit has
137		// something to walk). Pre-fix: the schema error caused audit to skip
138		// the install section entirely.
139		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(pluginRoot, "package.yaml"), []byte(`name: broken-plugin
140	version: 1.0.0
141	type: plugin
142	source: https://example.com/broken-plugin
143	api_version: 1
144	install:
145	  skills:
146	    - source: skills
147	      target: .agents/skills
148	`), 0o644))
149	
150		state := &registry.InstalledState{
151			Installed: []registry.InstalledEntry{
152				{
153					Name:    "broken-plugin",
154					Version: "1.0.0",
155					Type:    registry.PackageTypePlugin,
156					Source:  pluginRoot,
157					Files:   []string{pluginRoot},
158				},
159			},
160		}
161		require.NoError(t, registry.SaveState(state))
162	
163		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
164		output, err := executeWithStdoutCapture(t, factory.NewRootCommand(), "doctor", "--plugins")
165		require.NoError(t, err)
166	
167		assert.Contains(t, output, "missing required field `description`",
168			"manifest schema defect must be reported — the whole point of doctor --plugins")
169		assert.Contains(t, output, "missing SKILL.md",
170			"structural defect must ALSO surface — a schema error must not hide install-tree problems")
171		assert.Contains(t, output, "broken symlink",
172			"every structural issue must surface in the same pass — otherwise operators fix the schema and re-run, only to discover more")
173	}
174	
175	// TestDoctorPluginsFlagReportsLostExecutablePermission is the ddx-b1503915
176	// acceptance test for executable-bit validation. A plugin that declares a
177	// file under install.executable must surface an issue when that file loses
178	// its execute bit (chmod 0644). Relies on auditExecutable in
179	// cli/internal/registry/audit.go.
180	func TestDoctorPluginsFlagReportsLostExecutablePermission(t *testing.T) {
181		workDir := t.TempDir()
182		homeDir := t.TempDir()
183		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
184	
185		pluginRoot := filepath.Join(homeDir, ddxroot.DirName, "plugins", "exec-plugin")
186		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(pluginRoot, 0o755))
187	
188		// A hook script that MUST be executable per the manifest, installed
189		// without its execute bit (simulates a post-install chmod mishap or a
190		// tar extraction that dropped the mode bits).
191		hookPath := filepath.Join(pluginRoot, "hooks", "post-install.sh")
192		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(hookPath), 0o755))
193		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(hookPath, []byte("#!/bin/sh\necho ok\n"), 0o644))
194	
195		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(pluginRoot, "package.yaml"), []byte(`name: exec-plugin
196	version: 1.0.0
197	description: Plugin with an executable hook
198	type: plugin
199	source: https://example.com/exec-plugin
200	api_version: 1
201	install:
202	  executable:
203	    - hooks/post-install.sh
204	`), 0o644))
205	
206		state := &registry.InstalledState{
207			Installed: []registry.InstalledEntry{
208				{
209					Name:    "exec-plugin",
210					Version: "1.0.0",
211					Type:    registry.PackageTypePlugin,
212					Source:  pluginRoot,
213					Files:   []string{pluginRoot},
214				},
215			},
216		}
217		require.NoError(t, registry.SaveState(state))
218	
219		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
220		output, err := executeWithStdoutCapture(t, factory.NewRootCommand(), "doctor", "--plugins")
221		require.NoError(t, err)
222		assert.Contains(t, output, "lost execute permission",
223			"a declared executable that lost its mode bit must surface — hooks silently failing to run is the kind of drift doctor exists to catch")
224		assert.Contains(t, output, "post-install.sh")
225	}
226	
227	// TestDoctorPluginsFlagReportsOrphanSymlinkTarget is the ddx-b1503915
228	// acceptance test for orphan-target detection on symlinks inside the
229	// package tree. auditBrokenSymlinks walks the root and reports each symlink
230	// whose target does not resolve.
231	func TestDoctorPluginsFlagReportsOrphanSymlinkTarget(t *testing.T) {
232		workDir := t.TempDir()
233		homeDir := t.TempDir()
234		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
235	
236		pluginRoot := filepath.Join(homeDir, ddxroot.DirName, "plugins", "orphan-plugin")
237		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(pluginRoot, 0o755))
238		// A symlink that points somewhere that does not exist. This is the
239		// shape left behind when a prior plugin version's skill directory was
240		// pruned but the symlink in the current layout wasn't.
241		require.NoError(t, os.Symlink(filepath.Join(pluginRoot, "does-not-exist"), filepath.Join(pluginRoot, "dangling-link")))
242	
243		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(pluginRoot, "package.yaml"), []byte(`name: orphan-plugin
244	version: 1.0.0
245	description: Plugin with a dangling symlink
246	type: plugin
247	source: https://example.com/orphan-plugin
248	api_version: 1
249	`), 0o644))
250	
251		state := &registry.InstalledState{
252			Installed: []registry.InstalledEntry{
253				{
254					Name:    "orphan-plugin",
255					Version: "1.0.0",
256					Type:    registry.PackageTypePlugin,
257					Source:  pluginRoot,
258					Files:   []string{pluginRoot},
259				},
260			},
261		}
262		require.NoError(t, registry.SaveState(state))
263	
264		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
265		output, err := executeWithStdoutCapture(t, factory.NewRootCommand(), "doctor", "--plugins")
266		require.NoError(t, err)
267		assert.Contains(t, output, "broken symlink target",
268			"a symlink whose target does not resolve must surface with its target path")
269		assert.Contains(t, output, "dangling-link")
270	}
271	
272	// TestDoctor_ReportsInstallTopology verifies that `ddx doctor` output contains
273	// a "Global Install (...) — ok|missing" line and a "Project Install (...) —
274	// ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global" line, and that the project line reads
275	// "lazy-resolves-to-global" when only the global install is present.
276	func TestDoctor_ReportsInstallTopology(t *testing.T) {
277		xdgDir := t.TempDir()
278		homeDir := t.TempDir()
279		workDir := t.TempDir()
280		t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDir)
281		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
282	
283		// Create the global plugin dir to simulate a global install.
284		globalPluginDir := filepath.Join(xdgDir, "ddx", "global", "plugins", "ddx")
285		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(globalPluginDir, 0o755))
286	
287		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
288		output, err := executeWithStdoutCapture(t, factory.NewRootCommand(), "doctor")
289		require.NoError(t, err)
290	
291		// Both topology lines must appear in the output.
292		assert.Contains(t, output, "Global Install (", "Global Install line must appear in doctor output")
293		assert.Contains(t, output, "Project Install (", "Project Install line must appear in doctor output")
294	
295		// When only the global install exists, the project line must report lazy resolution.
296		assert.Contains(t, output, "lazy-resolves-to-global",
297			"Project Install line must read lazy-resolves-to-global when global install exists but project install does not")
298	
299		// The global install we created must report "ok".
300		assert.Contains(t, output, ") — ok", "Global Install must report ok when global plugin dir exists")
301	}
302	
303	func executeWithStdoutCapture(t *testing.T, root *cobra.Command, args ...string) (string, error) {
304		t.Helper()
305	
306		stdoutR, stdoutW, err := os.Pipe()
307		require.NoError(t, err)
308		stderrR, stderrW, err := os.Pipe()
309		require.NoError(t, err)
310	
311		origStdout := os.Stdout
312		origStderr := os.Stderr
313		os.Stdout = stdoutW
314		os.Stderr = stderrW
315		defer func() {
316			os.Stdout = origStdout
317			os.Stderr = origStderr
318		}()
319	
320		var outBuf bytes.Buffer
321		var errBuf bytes.Buffer
322		var wg sync.WaitGroup
323		wg.Add(2)
324		go func() {
325			defer wg.Done()
326			_, _ = io.Copy(&outBuf, stdoutR)
327		}()
328		go func() {
329			defer wg.Done()
330			_, _ = io.Copy(&errBuf, stderrR)
331		}()
332	
333		root.SetArgs(args)
334		err = root.Execute()
335	
336		_ = stdoutW.Close()
337		_ = stderrW.Close()
338		wg.Wait()
339	
340		return outBuf.String() + errBuf.String(), err
341	}
342

data.text:
All three ACs are satisfied by existing code — no new changes were needed:

1. **TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject** — passes; `performGlobalUpdate` in `update.go` routes `--global` exclusively to the XDG global plugin tree and never calls `refreshShippedSkills`.
2. **TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode** — passes (both `in-tree` and `convention` sub-tests); `performUpdate` calls `refreshShippedSkills(workingDir)` only, leaving the global tree untouched.
3. **TestDoctor_ReportsInstallTopology** — passes; `reportSkillInstallTopology` in `doctor.go` (line 418–449) prints the "Global Install" and "Project Install" lines with correct status tokens.

Written `no_changes_rationale.txt` with a `verification_command` so the orchestrator can confirm by exit code.
The background test run also completed with exit code 0. The `no_changes_rationale.txt` is in place with the verification command. Done.
